blocks.body: Present Simple: Adverbs of Frequency — always, usually, often, sometimes, never, and where these words go in a sentence.

{"level":"A1–A2","intro":"The words always, usually, often, sometimes, never show how often something happens.","rules":[{"label":"Before the main verb","color":"emerald","text":"With a normal verb, an adverb of frequency usually goes before it.","example":"She often walks to school."},{"label":"After be","color":"blue","text":"With be, these words usually go after be.","example":"He is usually tired after work."},{"label":"Meaning","color":"amber","text":"Choose the word by frequency, from always to never.","example":"always → usually → often → sometimes → never"}]}

{"title":"1. Common frequency words","intro":"Start with the most common words.","items":[{"label":"100%","title":"always","subtitle":"I always check my homework."},{"label":"often","title":"usually / often","subtitle":"We usually eat at home. / He often calls me."},{"label":"sometimes","title":"sometimes","subtitle":"She sometimes walks to work."},{"label":"0%","title":"never","subtitle":"They never drink coffee at night."}]}

{"title":"2. Where to put these words","sections":[{"label":"Before the main verb","color":"emerald","description":"In Present Simple, an adverb of frequency usually goes before a normal verb.","examples":[{"en":"I usually get up at seven.","ua":"I usually get up at seven."},{"en":"She often studies in the library.","ua":"She often studies in the library."}]},{"label":"After be","color":"blue","description":"If the sentence has am / is / are, put the frequency word after that form.","examples":[{"en":"He is always late.","ua":"He is always late."},{"en":"We are sometimes tired after class.","ua":"We are sometimes tired after class."}]},{"label":"In negatives","color":"amber","description":"The frequency word often goes after don't / doesn't and before the main verb.","examples":[{"en":"I don't usually eat breakfast early.","ua":"I don't usually eat breakfast early."},{"en":"She doesn't often go out on Mondays.","ua":"She doesn't often go out on Mondays."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Position in the sentence","intro":"Look at the usual word order.","rows":[{"en":"She often reads in the evening.","ua":"adverb before main verb","note":"often + reads"},{"en":"He is usually at home at six.","ua":"adverb after be","note":"is + usually"},{"en":"We sometimes eat out on Fridays.","ua":"adverb before main verb","note":"sometimes + eat"},{"en":"They never drive to work.","ua":"adverb before main verb","note":"never + drive"}],"warning":"After doesn't, the verb stays in the base form: She doesn't usually drink tea."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Putting the frequency word at the end of the sentence.","wrong":"❌ She goes to work by bus always.","right":"✅ She always goes to work by bus."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Not putting the frequency word after be.","wrong":"❌ He is late usually.","right":"✅ He is usually late."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Adding -s after doesn't.","wrong":"❌ She doesn't usually drinks tea.","right":"✅ She doesn't usually drink tea."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["always, usually, often, sometimes, never show the frequency of an action.","With a normal verb, the frequency word usually goes before the verb.","With be, the frequency word usually goes after be.","In negatives, we often see don't / doesn't + adverb + V1.","A common mistake is doesn't usually drinks instead of doesn't usually drink."]}
